title: Implement Verdict-First Review/Test Result Handling
description: Extend the worker result schema and runtime models to carry verdict, findings_count, and requires_followup for review/test outputs, then update scheduler finalization logic so structured verdict fields become the source of truth for review/test completion and blocking. Preserve a compatibility fallback for legacy outputs, emit an execution-history warning when fallback parsing is used, and leave a clear hook for disabling compat mode later.
acceptance_criteria: Agent output parsing accepts verdict, findings_count, and optional requires_followup alongside existing handoff fields.
Review and tester beads with verdict=approved complete even when remaining contains arbitrary prose.
Review and tester beads with verdict=needs_changes block and require block_reason.
Legacy review/test outputs without verdict still use the existing text-based fallback path, and that fallback records a warning in execution_history.
The implementation leaves an explicit compatibility control point so compat mode can be disabled in a future change without redesigning this flow.

handoff_summary.completed: Extended runtime result models and worker output schema to carry `verdict`, `findings_count`, and `requires_followup`. Updated scheduler finalization so review/test beads use structured verdicts as the primary completion/blocking signal, preserve legacy `remaining` parsing behind an explicit compat flag, and record an execution-history warning when that fallback path is used. Added targeted tests for approved verdicts with arbitrary prose, needs-changes blocking, and compat-warning fallback behavior.
handoff_summary.remaining: Prompt and guardrail template migration to require structured review/test verdict output is not included in this bead. Compat mode remains enabled via an explicit control point for a future follow-up change.
handoff_summary.risks: Legacy review/test agents that omit structured verdicts still depend on the compat fallback path until prompt/template migration is completed. Scheduler behavior now normalizes review/test outcomes from verdicts, so downstream code reading `last_agent_result` or `handoff_summary` should prefer the new structured fields.
handoff_summary.next_action: Run review against the scheduler/model changes, with attention to compat fallback semantics and any prompt-side follow-up needed to make verdict fields mandatory later.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Shared-risk area is `src/codex_orchestrator/scheduler.py` finalize logic and `tests/test_orchestrator.py` around review/test completion behavior. Downstream agents should not remove `REVIEW_TEST_VERDICT_COMPAT_MODE` or compat warning coverage unless they are intentionally finishing the prompt migration.

conflict_risks: Shared-risk area is `src/codex_orchestrator/scheduler.py` finalize logic and `tests/test_orchestrator.py` around review/test completion behavior. Downstream agents should not remove `REVIEW_TEST_VERDICT_COMPAT_MODE` or compat warning coverage unless they are intentionally finishing the prompt migration.

execution_history.summary: Implemented verdict-first review/test result handling with structured verdict fields, legacy fallback warnings, and focused regression coverage.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Run targeted validation needed to confirm the implementation.

Disallowed actions:
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
